claim: AI agents should save time and assist humans rather than fully replace human decision-making; share responsibility with the AI so humans can catch and correct hallucinations
actionable: When designing agent workflows, optimize for human-in-the-loop verification rather than full automation; have agents generate drafts for review instead of taking final actions.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Spending 5+ hours on planning and prototyping agents can save 20+ hours in development by preventing false starts and clarifying tool requirements upfront
actionable: Always dedicate time to planning agent architecture before implementation; create detailed specifications of agent goals, tools, and workflows.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Multi-agent systems suffer from compounding hallucination errors; if three agents each work 95% of the time, the whole system only works 86% of the time (0.95^3)
actionable: Calculate error compounding before building multi-agent systems; design critical paths to minimize agent dependencies and add guardrails between agents.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Input and output guardrails (lightweight LLM checks before/after agent execution) significantly reduce hallucinations; use simpler models like GPT-4.1 mini for guardrail validation tasks
actionable: Implement pre-execution guardrails to validate input sanity and post-execution guardrails to verify outputs match requirements; use cost-effective smaller models for these checks.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Specialized agents that handle distinct responsibilities (e.g., one for Slack, one for database) reduce hallucinations better than monolithic agents with too many tools
actionable: Decompose agent capabilities by domain; use an orchestrator agent to route tasks to specialized agents rather than overwhelming one agent with many tools.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Including concrete examples in system prompts (showing input-output pairs) is critical for agent consistency; examples should contain full details, line numbers, and formatting
actionable: Add detailed examples to your system prompt showing exact input/output format; include metadata, line numbers, and formatting details agents should replicate.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Long system prompts should use positive directives instead of negatives; LLMs frequently drop negations in longer prompts (e.g., 'use simple language' vs 'don't use complex language')
actionable: Reframe all instructions in system prompts positively; instead of 'don't X', say 'do Y'; this becomes critical in prompts longer than 3-4 paragraphs.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: System prompt contradictions cause inconsistent agent behavior; contradicting instructions about tone, style, or behavior lead to the agent randomly choosing between conflicting directives
actionable: Review system prompts for implicit contradictions across paragraphs; test agent responses to verify consistent behavior and resolve any conflicting instructions.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Version control system prompts like code; reverting to previous versions helps identify introduced contradictions or excessive length that degrades performance
actionable: Maintain a version history of system prompts in Git or a prompt management tool like Langfuse; track performance changes when making updates.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Swapping LLMs, even to supposedly 'better' models, requires re-testing and re-tuning system prompts; different models interpret prompts and handle tools differently
actionable: When upgrading models, test the full agent workflow thoroughly; don't assume benchmarks translate to real-world agent performance; be prepared to adjust system prompts.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Different LLMs excel at different tasks; Claude 3.7 Sonnet is strongest for coding, Gemini 2.5 Pro is stronger for creative writing agents; test rather than defaulting to one favorite
actionable: Profile each LLM with your agent's use cases; maintain a reference table of which models work best for different agent types (coding vs. creative vs. reasoning).
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Context length limits cause system prompts to be dropped from conversation history, leading to agents forgetting their core instructions mid-conversation
actionable: Monitor context usage in long conversations; implement conversation summarization or reset strategies when approaching context limits; avoid local LLMs with small limits for complex agents.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Agents repeat previous hallucinations in the same conversation; even after correction, agents may revert to earlier mistakes in subsequent messages
actionable: Start fresh conversations frequently with agents; don't rely on mid-conversation corrections persisting; implement long-term memory mechanisms to preserve corrections across sessions.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Long-term memory for agents is functionally equivalent to RAG; both use vector databases and embeddings; RAG optimization techniques (query expansion, re-ranking) apply to memory systems
actionable: Apply RAG best practices to agent memory systems; use query expansion and re-ranking to improve memory retrieval accuracy in long-running agents.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Include tool call requests and responses in conversation history; agents can re-reference tool results in follow-up messages, reducing redundant tool calls and improving accuracy
actionable: Store complete tool call invocations and responses in conversation history; design tools to return formatted metadata so agents can reliably reference results later.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Tool descriptions should be detailed and include examples of parameter formats; provide examples in tool descriptions especially for complex parameters
actionable: Write detailed tool descriptions with example parameter formats; show agents exactly how to structure queries or requests to your tools.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Wrap tools in try-catch blocks and return specific error messages to agents; this allows agents to retry with corrected parameters instead of failing silently
actionable: Implement error handling in all tools; return descriptive error messages that agents can use to self-correct their tool invocations.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Return only the information agents need from tools; strip metadata from API responses; format results as well-structured markdown or text to improve parsing
actionable: Clean tool responses before passing to agents; remove unnecessary API metadata; format data with clear structure (markdown, numbered lists) for better agent comprehension.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents

claim: Tool descriptions specify how to use individual tools; system prompts specify how to use multiple tools together; separate these concerns clearly
actionable: In tool descriptions, explain what the tool does and its parameters; in system prompts, explain workflows that combine tools to solve higher-level goals.
source_title: My Top 20 Lessons from Building 100s of AI Agents
